originalTitle: The young women who fought in Ethiopia's last civil war and don't want to see another one
title: Brave Girls Who Want Peace in Ethiopia
content:
Hello from Ethiopia!

Hello, my little friends! I’m your news reporter from beautiful Africa. Today, we are going on a journey to a country called Ethiopia. Ethiopia is a big country in Africa with many friendly people and amazing animals. It has tall mountains and wide plains. There are many different tribes and languages. People love to drink coffee and eat yummy food there! Did you know? Ethiopia is one of the oldest countries in the world! It has a very long and interesting history. In Ethiopia, there is a special place called Tigray. Tigray is like a big region or state, full of homes and schools and farms. It's a place where many children like you live, play, and learn every day. People there work hard and care for their families.

What is a "War"?

Sometimes, grown-ups have big, big disagreements. When these disagreements get very bad, it can turn into something called a "war." A war means people fight with each other, sometimes with loud noises and scary things. It is very sad. War makes people feel scared. It can break homes and schools. Children might not be able to go to school or play outside. Friends and families might get separated. Nobody likes war because it brings a lot of sadness and hurts many people. It is the opposite of peace. Did you know? Even brave soldiers don't like war. They want peace too, so everyone can be safe.

Brave Young Women

In Tigray, not so long ago, there was a big fight, a war, in their country. During this time, some young women had to be very, very brave. They were like the big sisters and aunties in the community. They had to stand up and try to protect their homes and their families. These young women were not regular soldiers, but they had to learn to be strong. They had to join the fight, even if they were scared. They wanted to help their people. It was a very hard time for them. They missed their normal lives, like going to school or playing with friends. They are heroes because they showed great courage. Courage means being brave even when you are very, very afraid. These women had to be courageous every single day during the war.

When Life Was Hard

The war made life very difficult for these brave young women and everyone in Tigray. Their lives changed a lot. Many homes were broken, so families had nowhere safe to sleep. Schools were closed, so children could not learn their lessons. Imagine if your school closed and you couldn't see your friends or learn new things! That's how it was for many. Food and water became very hard to find. It was a time of hunger and sadness for many, many people. These young women tell us that the war "destroyed their lives." This means it made them very sad, took away their happiness, and changed everything they knew. They lost many friends and felt very lonely. Their dreams for the future seemed far away. Did you know? When homes are broken in a war, people sometimes have to live in tents or with relatives far away from their usual homes.

Hoping for Peace

After a long time, the fighting in Tigray stopped. Everyone breathed a big sigh of relief. Now, these brave young women want one thing more than anything else: peace. What is peace? Peace is when everyone is friendly. It means no more fighting, no more loud noises, and no more fear. It means children can go to school every day and play outside safely. It means families can be together and laugh and share food. These women really want peace to last forever. They remember how awful the war was. They want all the children in Tigray to grow up happy and safe, never knowing the sadness of war. They want to rebuild their homes and their lives. Did you know? The symbol for peace is often a dove, a beautiful white bird.

Worries About Tomorrow

Even though the fighting has stopped, some people in Tigray are still a little worried. They remember how easily the war started. They are afraid that the fighting might start again. This is called "fears grow that fighting could resume." It means people are worried that the bad times might come back. These young women who fought in the war do not want to see another one. They know how much pain it brings. They want to keep the peace strong. They want to make sure everyone works hard to stay friends and talk out their problems instead of fighting. It’s like when you have a fun play day, but then you worry your friend might get mad and not play with you tomorrow. It’s a sad feeling. They want to make sure everyone is happy and calm.

Learning from the Past

These brave young women teach us a very important lesson. They teach us that war is very, very bad, and peace is very, very good. They show us that it takes courage to fight for your people, but it takes even more courage to work for peace after the fighting stops. We can all learn from their story. We can learn to be kind to each other. We can learn to share our toys and listen to our friends. When we have a problem, we can talk about it instead of shouting or fighting. This is how we build peace in our own homes and schools. Everyone in the world wants to live in peace. No child wants to be scared.

Let's Choose Peace!

So, what can we do? We can listen to these brave young women from Ethiopia. We can remember their stories. We can choose to be kind. We can choose to be friends. We can choose to talk instead of fight. Every little act of kindness helps make the world a more peaceful place. Let's all work together for a world where everyone can play, learn, and grow up happy and safe. Let's choose peace, every day!
summary: Young women in Ethiopia who were part of a war want everyone to live in peace and hope fighting never starts again.
whyMatters: It is important because wars make people sad and hurt, and everyone deserves to live safely and happily without fighting.
